title: Media
description: A media attachment — photograph, video, or document scan. Used for identification, valuation, condition documentation, and provenance records.

url.description: URI pointing to the media file. May be an HTTPS URL, S3 URI, or other storage reference.

caption.description: Human-readable description of what this media shows.
examples: Front view of the engagement ring
Walk-through video of the model railway layout
Scan of the GIA diamond certificate

mediaType.description: MIME type of the media file. Maps to Schema.org encodingFormat.
mediaType.$comment: Common values: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp (photos), video/mp4, video/quicktime (videos), application/pdf (document scans, certificates).

viewType.description: The purpose of this media — what question does it answer? Enables executors and dealers to find specific documentation without scrolling through galleries.
viewType.$comment: overview: general view showing the whole item in context. identification: close-up of the feature that identifies this specific item (catalogue number, hallmark, label). condition: shows current condition, wear, patina. provenance: documentation of ownership history (receipts, auction records). maker_mark: manufacturer's mark, stamp, or signature. serial_number: close-up of serial/chassis/reference number. damage: specific damage, repairs, or defects. scale_reference: item photographed alongside a ruler or known object for size. label: product label, wine label, stamp on reverse. certificate: valuation certificate, authenticity certificate, gemological report. receipt: purchase receipt or invoice. environment: the storage environment (wine cellar temperature, gun cabinet, display case).

takenAt.description: When this media was captured. Useful for establishing condition at a point in time.

thumbnailUrl.description: URI for a smaller preview version of the media. Maps to Schema.org thumbnail.
